title: Title
noOpportunities: No opportunities yet. Create your first one!
tooltip_marketAttractiveness: How large and growing is the target market? Consider market size, growth rate, and customer demand.
tooltip_strategicFit: How well does this opportunity align with our core competencies, strategy, and long-term goals?
tooltip_feasibility: Can we realistically deliver this? Consider technical complexity, required resources, and timeline.
tooltip_commercialViability: Is there a viable business model? Consider pricing potential, margins, and customer willingness to pay.
tooltip_risk: What is the overall risk level? Consider market, technical, regulatory, and execution risks. Lower score = lower risk = better.
revertConfirm: Are you sure you want to go back to the previous stage?
moveToBusinessCase: Move to Implementation and GTM Plan
detailedAnalysisPlaceholder: Provide a thorough analysis covering key aspects, evidence, and conclusions...
targetCustomersPlaceholder: Describe target customer segments, their size, and key characteristics...
customerRelationshipPlaceholder: Describe existing relationships, partnerships, LOIs, or pilot programs...
competitorsPlaceholder: List key competitors, their solutions, and market position...
competitivePositionPlaceholder: Describe your competitive advantages and differentiation...
maTamDescPlaceholder: Describe what the TAM includes — which segments, geographies, and use cases define the total addressable market...
maSamDescPlaceholder: Describe how the SAM is derived from the TAM — which segments are realistically serviceable and why...
maNoSegments: No customer segments defined. Add segments to visualize your target market.
maNoCompetitors: No competitors defined. Add competitors to build your competitive landscape.
maNoRegions: No regions defined. Add regions to map your geographical strategy.
guidance_sf_1: How well does this align with our core competencies and existing capabilities?
guidance_sf_2: Does it support our long-term strategic goals and growth roadmap?
guidance_sf_3: Are there synergies with our existing product portfolio or customer base?
guidance_fe_1: Do we have the technical expertise and resources to deliver this?
guidance_fe_2: What is the realistic timeline for development and market readiness?
guidance_fe_3: Are there critical dependencies on external partners or technologies?
guidance_cv_1: Is there a viable and scalable business model with attractive margins?
guidance_cv_2: What is the customer's willingness to pay and the pricing power?
guidance_cv_3: How quickly can this reach profitability and positive cash flow?
guidance_ri_1: What are the key market, technical, and regulatory risks?
guidance_ri_2: How can risks be mitigated through partnerships, phasing, or insurance?
guidance_ri_3: What is the worst-case scenario and its impact on the organization?
sfNoGaps: No capability gaps identified. Add gaps to track required capabilities.
sfActionPlaceholder: Describe the action plan to close this gap (hiring, partnerships, training...)
cvBreakEvenUnitsPlaceholder: Number of units/customers needed to break even
feNoMilestones: No milestones yet. Add milestones to track your development timeline.
riNoItems: No risks identified yet. Add risks to build your risk matrix.
saDescPlaceholder: Describe the current positioning and context...
saRationalePlaceholder: Explain the rationale behind this classification...
saVcNoStages: No value chain stages defined. Add stages to map the industry value chain.
saVcDiffPlaceholder: What differentiates players in this stage? What creates customer value?
saVcDynPlaceholder: What trends, disruptions, or shifts are happening at this stage?
saCustSegNoEntries: No customer segments defined yet. Add segments to map your target customers.
saCustSegNeedsPlaceholder: What are the primary needs and pain points of this segment?
saCustSegWtpPlaceholder: Price sensitivity, budget range, purchasing behavior...
saCompNoEntries: No competitors analyzed yet. Add competitors to build your competitive landscape.
saCustIntNoEntries: No customer interviews documented yet. Add interviews to capture customer insights.
saCustIntInsightsPlaceholder: Main takeaways and learnings from this interview...
saCustIntPainPointsPlaceholder: Specific pain points and challenges mentioned...
saCustIntQuotesPlaceholder: Direct quotes that capture the customer's perspective...
saBizValuePropPlaceholder: What unique value do we deliver to the customer?
saBizCustSegPlaceholder: Who are our most important customers?
saBizChannelsPlaceholder: How do we reach and deliver value to customers?
saBizCustRelPlaceholder: What type of relationship do we establish?
saBizRevenuePlaceholder: How does the business earn revenue?
saBizKeyResPlaceholder: What key resources does our value proposition require?
saBizKeyActPlaceholder: What key activities does our value proposition require?
saBizKeyPartPlaceholder: Who are our key partners and suppliers?
saBizCostStrPlaceholder: What are the most important costs inherent in our business model?
saLcSolutionPlaceholder: Top 1-3 features or capabilities that solve the problems...
saLcUvpPlaceholder: Single, clear, compelling message that turns a visitor into a customer...
saLcUnfairAdvPlaceholder: Something that cannot be easily copied or bought (e.g., insider knowledge, network effects)...
saLcKeyMetricsPlaceholder: Key numbers that tell you how your business is doing...
saLcChannelsPlaceholder: Path to customers (inbound, outbound, direct, partnerships)...
saLcCostStrPlaceholder: Customer acquisition costs, hosting, people, etc...
saVpcCustJobsPlaceholder: What tasks are customers trying to get done? What problems are they trying to solve?
saVpcCustPainsPlaceholder: What negative emotions, undesired costs, or risks does the customer experience?
saVpcCustGainsPlaceholder: What outcomes and benefits does the customer want or would delight them?
saVpcProductsPlaceholder: Which products and services do we offer to help customers get their jobs done?
saVpcPainRelieversPlaceholder: How do our products and services alleviate customer pains?
saVpcGainCreatorsPlaceholder: How do our products and services create customer gains?
saCbaFunctionalPlaceholder: What practical, utilitarian benefits does the customer receive?
saCbaEmotionalPlaceholder: What positive feelings does the customer experience when using the product?
saCbaSocialPlaceholder: How does the product help the customer in social contexts (status, belonging)?
saCbaSelfExpressivePlaceholder: How does the product help customers express who they are or want to be?
saTcmOurValuePlaceholder: What value do we deliver to customers?
saTcmCompValuePlaceholder: What value do competitors deliver to the same customers?
saTcmCustNeedsPlaceholder: What do customers truly need and want?
saTcmOurUniquePlaceholder: Value only we provide — not competitors, aligned with customer needs...
saTcmTheirUniquePlaceholder: Value only competitors provide — our gap to close...
saTcmCommonPlaceholder: Value both we and competitors deliver — points of parity...
saTcmUnmetPlaceholder: Customer needs neither we nor competitors address — white space opportunities...
saPosRtbPlaceholder: Evidence and proof points that support the key benefit...
saPosDiffPlaceholder: Our product is the only one that [key differentiator]...
saPosStatementPlaceholder: For [target audience] who [need], [product] is a [category] that [key benefit]. Unlike [alternative], we [differentiator] because [reason to believe].
saHorizon1Desc: Maintain & defend the core business. Incremental improvements to existing products/markets.
saHorizon2Desc: Nurture emerging businesses. New capabilities that extend current business model.
saHorizon3Desc: Create viable future options. Exploratory ventures, new markets, disruptive innovation.
clNoRatings: Rate competitors on each dimension to see the spider chart.
dsOrCulturePlaceholder: How ready is the organization for this change? Cultural fit, change management needs...
dsOrProcessesPlaceholder: What process changes or system integrations are required? ERP, CRM, supply chain...
dsOrSkillsPlaceholder: Do we have the required skills? Training needs, hiring requirements, knowledge gaps...
dsOrLeadershipPlaceholder: Is there executive sponsorship? Governance structure, decision-making clarity...
dsOrResourcesPlaceholder: Budget, headcount, infrastructure — are resources allocated or obtainable?
dsOrStakeholdersPlaceholder: Key stakeholders identified? Level of buy-in, potential resistance, communication plan...
dsPilotNoCustomers: No pilot customers yet. Add target customers to track validation progress.
dsPilotValidationPlaceholder: What was confirmed? Need, willingness to pay, use case fit...
dsPilotNotesPlaceholder: Overall observations on pilot customer approach, next steps...
dsPfDimNotesPlaceholder: How does this dimension relate to the existing portfolio?
dsPfCrossSellingPlaceholder: What cross-selling opportunities exist with existing products? Which customer segments can be addressed?
dsPfCannibalizationPlaceholder: Could this product cannibalize existing products? Which revenue streams are at risk?
dsPfSharedResourcesPlaceholder: Which resources, platforms, or infrastructure can be shared with existing products?
dsPfNotesPlaceholder: General observations on portfolio fit, strategic considerations...
dsPfIdeaScoringNote: Note: Portfolio synergy was already assessed in Idea Scoring (sf_portfolio_synergy). This tab provides a deeper analysis.
guideProcessDesc: The BD Pipeline follows a structured Stage Gate process to systematically evaluate and advance business opportunities. Each stage builds upon the previous one, with gate reviews ensuring only the most promising opportunities proceed.
guideStage_idea: Initial capture of a new business opportunity. Basic information such as title, industry, geography, and technology is recorded.
guideStage_rough_scoring: Quick evaluation across five key criteria (1–5 scale). Helps prioritize which ideas deserve deeper analysis.
guideStage_gate1: First formal review. Evaluates the rough scoring and decides: Go, Hold, or No-Go.
guideStage_detailed_scoring: In-depth analysis across nine dimensions: Market Potential, Customer Landscape, Competitor Landscape, Strategic Fit, Technical Feasibility, Organisational Readiness, Commercial Viability, Risk, and Pilot Customer validation. Supported by 17 strategic analysis frameworks.
guideStage_gate2: Second review. Requires strong evidence from detailed scoring and strategic analyses to proceed.
guideStage_investment_case: Investment Calculation Sheet: Full financial modeling including investment costs, R&D expenses, revenue projections, EBIT, ROCE, NPV (WACC-based), payback period, and accumulated cash flow analysis.
guideStage_gate3: Third review. Evaluates the financial business case and decides whether to proceed to implementation and go-to-market.
guideStage_business_case: Implementation and GTM Plan: Go-to-market strategy, pilot customer engagement, lead generation, and commercial execution planning.
guideStage_implement_review: Active implementation phase with structured review cycles to track progress, gather learnings, and refine the approach.
guideScoringDesc: Each opportunity is evaluated across five weighted categories. Use the tables below to ensure consistent, standardized scoring across all evaluators.
guideRiskInverted: Note: Risk scoring is inverted — a score of 1 (low risk) is best, 5 (high risk) is worst.
guideMA_desc: Evaluates the overall attractiveness and size of the target market. In the Detailed Scoring, this is broken into three separate tabs: Market Potential, Customer Landscape, and Competitor Landscape.
guideMA_sub1_desc: 5-year TAM and SAM projections with automated CAGR calculation. Includes market maturity assessment and growth drivers.
guideMA_sub2_desc: Target customer segments with size, needs, and priority. Customer relationship assessment and willingness to pay.
guideMA_sub3_desc: Competitor market share distribution, threat levels, and competitive position analysis. Barriers to entry.
guideMA_sub4_desc: Regional market potential ratings (1–5), market size per region, and regulatory/logistical notes.
guideMA_s1: Very small market (<€1M TAM), declining, highly fragmented with dominant incumbents
guideMA_s2: Small market (€1–10M TAM), flat growth, several strong competitors
guideMA_s3: Medium market (€10–50M TAM), moderate growth (5–10%), manageable competition
guideMA_s4: Large market (€50–200M TAM), strong growth (10–20%), favorable competitive position
guideMA_s5: Very large market (>€200M TAM), rapid growth (>20%), clear market gap or first-mover advantage
guideSF_desc: Assesses how well the opportunity aligns with the company's strategic direction, core competencies, and existing portfolio. Uses an Alignment Radar and Capability Gap Analysis.
guideSF_sub1_desc: Visual radar chart comparing current vs. required levels across: Core Competency, Strategy, Portfolio Synergy, Customer Base, Technology Fit, and Channel Fit.
guideSF_sub2_desc: Identifies gaps between current and required capability levels, with prioritized action plans (high/medium/low).
guideSF_sub3_desc: Potential synergies with existing products, customers, or channels.
guideSF_s1: No alignment with strategy; requires entirely new capabilities; no synergies
guideSF_s2: Weak alignment; significant capability gaps; minimal synergies
guideSF_s3: Moderate alignment; some capabilities exist; limited synergies
guideSF_s4: Strong alignment; most capabilities available; clear synergies
guideSF_s5: Perfect strategic fit; leverages core strengths; strong portfolio synergies
guideFE_desc: Evaluates the technical feasibility of realizing the opportunity. Features a TRL Gauge (1–9) and interactive development milestone timeline.
guideFE_sub1_desc: Visual gauge from basic research (TRL 1) to proven system in operational environment (TRL 9).
guideFE_sub2_desc: Interactive timeline with milestones, target dates, and status tracking (planned, in progress, completed, delayed).
guideFE_sub3_desc: Availability of required personnel, equipment, infrastructure, and realistic project timeline.
guideFE_s1: Fundamental research needed (TRL 1–2); no resources available; >5 years to market
guideFE_s2: Early development (TRL 3–4); major resource gaps; 3–5 years to market
guideFE_s3: Prototype stage (TRL 5–6); some resources available; 2–3 years to market
guideFE_s4: Demonstration phase (TRL 7–8); resources largely available; 1–2 years to market
guideFE_s5: Market-ready (TRL 9); all resources in place; <1 year to market
guideCV_desc: Analyzes the financial attractiveness with 5-year revenue/cost projections, unit-based break-even analysis, and margin KPI cards.
guideCV_sub1_desc: Pricing strategy documentation (SaaS, hardware, RaaS, etc.), unit price, and revenue generation approach.
guideCV_sub2_desc: Gross margin percentage with visual KPI card. Cost structure analysis and path to profitability.
guideCV_sub3_desc: Annual revenue/cost projections with chart visualization. Unit-based break-even analysis.
guideCV_s1: No clear revenue model; negative margins expected; no path to profitability
guideCV_s2: Unclear pricing; thin margins (<10%); break-even >5 years
guideCV_s3: Defined pricing model; moderate margins (10–25%); break-even in 3–5 years
guideCV_s4: Strong pricing power; good margins (25–40%); break-even in 1–3 years
guideCV_s5: Premium pricing justified; excellent margins (>40%); break-even <1 year
guideRI_desc: Identifies and assesses risks using a 5×5 Risk Matrix (probability × impact) and a structured Risk Register with mitigation strategies. Lower risk scores are better.
guideRI_sub1_desc: Visual probability × impact matrix. Risks plotted by category (market, technical, regulatory, execution, financial).
guideRI_sub2_desc: Structured list of identified risks with category, probability (1–5), impact (1–5), and mitigation strategy.
guideRI_sub3_desc: Investment risk, funding uncertainty, and currency/economic exposure.
guideRI_s1: Minimal risk across all dimensions; stable regulatory environment; proven technology
guideRI_s2: Low risk; minor regulatory concerns; well-understood technology
guideRI_s3: Moderate risk; some regulatory uncertainty; manageable technical challenges
guideRI_s4: High risk; significant regulatory hurdles; unproven technology; financial uncertainty
guideRI_s5: Very high risk; hostile regulatory environment; breakthrough technology required; major financial exposure
guideCL_desc: Evaluates the depth of understanding of target customers, their needs, pain points, and buying behavior.
guideCL_sub1_desc: Clarity and specificity of defined target customer segments.
guideCL_sub2_desc: Understanding of customer problems, unmet needs, and jobs-to-be-done.
guideCL_sub3_desc: Knowledge of decision-making process, buying criteria, and willingness to pay.
guideCL_s1: No customer understanding; target segments undefined
guideCL_s2: Basic assumptions about customers; limited validation
guideCL_s3: Some customer research done; segments partially validated
guideCL_s4: Strong customer understanding; validated segments with clear needs
guideCL_s5: Deep customer insight; fully validated segments, strong relationships established
guideCompL_desc: Assesses the competitive environment, key players, their strengths, and your differentiation potential.
guideCompL_sub1_desc: Number and strength of existing and potential competitors.
guideCompL_sub2_desc: Uniqueness of your offering vs. alternatives and substitutes.
guideCompL_sub3_desc: Difficulty for new competitors to enter; your defensibility.
guideCompL_s1: Saturated market; strong incumbents; no clear differentiation
guideCompL_s2: Many competitors; limited differentiation; low barriers
guideCompL_s4: Few direct competitors; clear differentiators; moderate barriers
guideCompL_s5: Blue ocean opportunity; strong differentiation; high barriers to entry
guideOR_desc: Evaluates whether the organisation has the culture, skills, processes, and resources to successfully execute the opportunity.
guideOR_sub1_desc: Organisational openness to change and innovation culture.
guideOR_sub2_desc: Availability of required technical and business skills.
guideOR_sub3_desc: Existence of supporting processes, leadership buy-in, and resource availability.
guideOR_sub4_desc: Level of support and alignment among key stakeholders.
guideOR_s1: Organisation unprepared; no skills, resources, or stakeholder support
guideOR_s2: Significant gaps in readiness; major capability building needed
guideOR_s3: Moderate readiness; some gaps to address but manageable
guideOR_s4: Good readiness; minor adjustments needed; stakeholders supportive
guideOR_s5: Fully prepared; strong capabilities, resources, and stakeholder alignment in place
guideStrategicDesc: The tool provides 16 strategic analysis frameworks organized into four groups: Market & Strategy, Market Research, Business Modelling, and Competitive Positioning. Each framework is linked to relevant Detailed Scoring dimensions via 'Related Strategic Analyses' badges.
guideAnsoffDesc: Maps growth strategies by combining existing/new products with existing/new markets. Helps classify the opportunity's growth approach (Market Penetration, Product Development, Market Development, or Diversification).
guideBcgDesc: Portfolio analysis tool that classifies opportunities by relative market share and market growth rate into Stars, Cash Cows, Question Marks, or Dogs.
guideMckinseyDesc: 9-cell matrix evaluating opportunities along industry attractiveness and competitive strength. Guides invest/selectivity/harvest decisions.
guideSwotDesc: Systematic analysis of internal Strengths and Weaknesses alongside external Opportunities and Threats.
guidePestelDesc: Macro-environmental analysis covering Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, and Legal factors.
guidePorterDesc: Analyzes five competitive forces: rivalry, new entrants, substitutes, buyer power, and supplier power to assess industry profitability.
guideValueChainDesc: Maps the industry value chain from raw materials to end customer. Evaluates each stage for margin attractiveness, differentiation factors, and industry dynamics.
guideCustSegDesc: Tracks customer segments with size, needs, willingness to pay, and priority level. Feeds into Customer Landscape scoring.
guideCompAnalysisDesc: Structured competitor profiling: strengths, weaknesses, market share, strategy, and threat level. Feeds into Competitor Landscape scoring.
guideCustIntDesc: Captures qualitative customer interview insights: key findings, pain points, and direct quotes. Validates assumptions with real customer voice.
guideBizModelDesc: Interactive Business Model Canvas (Osterwalder): 9 building blocks from value proposition to cost structure.
guideLeanCanvasDesc: Lean Canvas (Ash Maurya): problem-solution fit focused template for early-stage opportunities. Emphasizes unfair advantage and key metrics.
guideVpcDesc: Value Proposition Canvas: maps customer jobs, pains, and gains against products, pain relievers, and gain creators.
guideCbaDesc: Customer Benefit Analysis: evaluates functional, emotional, social, and self-expressive benefits of the opportunity.
guideTcmDesc: Three Circle Model: visualizes the overlap between our value, competitor value, and customer needs to identify unique differentiation.
guidePosDesc: Positioning Statement: structured framework for target audience, category, key benefit, reason to believe, and competitive differentiation.
guideDetailedDesc: Each opportunity is evaluated across eight detailed scoring dimensions. Each dimension has specialized tools and visualizations, and is linked to relevant strategic analysis frameworks.
guideDS_marketDesc: 5-year TAM and SAM projections with automated CAGR calculation, geographical focus with regional potential ratings, and market growth analysis.
guideDS_customerDesc: Target customer profiling, customer relationship assessment, and customer segment pie chart visualization with size and description.
guideDS_competitorDesc: Competitor market share visualization, threat level assessment, and competitive position analysis.
guideDS_strategicDesc: Alignment radar chart across 6 dimensions (core competency, strategy, portfolio, customer base, technology, channel) and capability gap analysis.
guideDS_feasibilityDesc: Technology Readiness Level (TRL 1–9) gauge assessment and interactive development milestone timeline with status tracking.
guideDS_orgReadinessDesc: Evaluates organizational preparedness across culture, processes, skills, leadership, resources, and stakeholder alignment.
guideDS_commercialDesc: 5-year revenue/cost projections, unit-based break-even analysis, pricing model documentation, and margin KPI cards.
guideDS_riskDesc: 5×5 risk matrix (probability × impact), structured risk register with categories (market, technical, regulatory, execution, financial), and mitigation strategies.
guideDS_pilotCustomerDesc: Track target pilot customers with contact status progression (identified → contacted → interested → LoI/confirmed), validation results, qualitative feedback, and an overall pilot readiness score (1–5).
guideGtmDesc: The Go-to-Market section is organized into three sub-areas to support the full journey from launch strategy to customer acquisition.
guideGtmStrategyDesc: Core GTM planning: target segments, channels, pricing strategy, key partners, KPIs, launch date, and a readiness checklist with progress tracking.
guideGtmPilotDesc: Track pilot agreements with customers including scope, timeline, status (planned/active/completed/cancelled), success criteria, and results. Builds on the Pilot Customer validation from Detailed Scoring.
guideGtmLeadGenDesc: Plan and track lead generation across channels (with target vs. actual leads and conversion rates), manage activities with status tracking, and monitor pipeline health with summary KPIs.
guideFormulaDesc: The total score is a weighted average of all five criteria. Risk is inverted (6 minus score) so that low-risk opportunities score higher.
guideWeightNote_MA: Highest weight — market size drives opportunity value
guideWeightNote_SF: Highest weight — strategic alignment is critical
guideWeightNote_CV: Medium weight — must be commercially viable
guideWeightNote_RI: Lowest weight — inverted: score is subtracted from 6
gtmTargetSegmentsPlaceholder: Which customer segments will you target first? Describe ICP, verticals, geographies...
gtmChannelsPlaceholder: Sales channels, marketing channels, distribution partners...
gtmPricingStrategyPlaceholder: Pricing model, tiers, introductory offers, competitive positioning...
gtmKpisPlaceholder: Revenue targets, customer acquisition goals, market share objectives...
gtmNoItems: No checklist items yet. Add items to track your launch readiness.
gtmNotesPlaceholder: Any additional notes, assumptions, or open questions...
gtmPilotEmpty: No pilot agreements yet. Add pilot customer engagements to track execution.
gtmPilotNotesPlaceholder: Overall observations on pilot programs, next steps...
gtmLeadNoChannels: No lead channels yet. Add channels to plan your lead generation strategy.
gtmLeadNoActivities: No activities yet. Add activities to track your lead generation efforts.
gtmLeadPipelineNotesPlaceholder: Notes on pipeline health, conversion optimization, bottlenecks...
irProgressNotesPlaceholder: Current status of implementation, milestones reached, blockers...
irLessonsLearnedPlaceholder: What worked well? What didn't? Key insights for future projects...
irNextStepsPlaceholder: Immediate actions, upcoming milestones, decisions needed...
irNoItems: No action items yet. Add items to track implementation tasks.
